seoTitle: How to Unprotect a Sheet in Microsoft Excel
metaDescription: Unlock a protected Excel worksheet through the Review tab, enter a password if one is set, and restore full editing access to your data and formulas.
intro: Grayed-out formatting options and blocked cell errors usually mean a worksheet has protection turned on. This guide covers finding the Unprotect Sheet command in the Review tab, entering a password if the sheet is encrypted, and confirming your data is fully editable afterward.

steps.heading: Spot the protection indicators
steps.body: Double-click an active cell and note any error prompt that confirms the sheet is protected.

steps.heading: Open the Review tab
steps.body: Move to the ribbon menu and click into the Review tab.

steps.heading: Click Unprotect Sheet
steps.body: Find the Protect tool section and click Unprotect Sheet.

steps.heading: Enter the password if required
steps.body: If the sheet is encrypted, type the security key into the pop-up field and click OK.

steps.heading: Try the Home tab alternative
steps.body: You can also find this option under the Home tab's Cells section by opening the Format dropdown and selecting Unprotect Sheet.

steps.heading: Check workbook-level locks
steps.body: If structural changes like adding sheets are also blocked, click Unprotect Workbook as well.

faq.question: What if I don't know the password for a protected sheet?
faq.answer: You'll need to enter the correct security key to unprotect it, so reach out to whoever originally set the password if you don't have it.

faq.question: Can I let others edit certain cells without fully unprotecting the sheet?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Protect Sheet dialog to check specific permission boxes that allow limited formatting or editing while protection stays active.

faq.question: Is unprotecting a sheet different from unprotecting a workbook?
faq.answer: Yes, sheet protection controls individual cell locks, while workbook protection controls structural changes like adding or removing sheets.

faq.question: How do I confirm the sheet is fully unlocked?
faq.answer: Double-click into your columns and rows to verify that typing, filtering, and formatting changes all work without errors.
